goal.goalText: I want to learn React and build modern web apps

skills.name: JavaScript Core
skills.description: ES6+, closures, async/await, prototypes, event loop
skills.reason: JavaScript mastery is the foundation before any React work

skills.name: React Fundamentals
skills.description: Components, JSX, props, state, hooks, lifecycle
skills.reason: Core React is the primary focus of your goal

skills.name: CSS & Styling
skills.description: Flexbox, Grid, Tailwind CSS, responsive design, animations
skills.reason: Modern web apps require polished, responsive UI

skills.name: State Management
skills.description: Context API, Redux Toolkit, Zustand, data flow patterns
skills.reason: Real-world apps need scalable state management beyond useState

skills.name: Frontend Tooling & Deployment
skills.description: Vite, Webpack, CI/CD, Vercel, performance optimization
skills.reason: Shipping a modern web app requires understanding the full dev pipeline

learningPlan.objective: Master ES6+ syntax and destructuring patterns
Build confidence with async/await and Promises
Understand closures and the event loop deeply
Review and reinforce all JS fundamentals
Write JSX confidently and understand component structure
Build stateful components using useState and useEffect
Master props, component composition and data flow
Implement React Router for multi-page navigation
Build a complete mini-app combining all React skills
Design fluid layouts using Flexbox and CSS Grid
Build responsive UIs with Tailwind CSS utility classes
Apply animations and transitions for polished UX
Share state across components using Context API
Manage complex state with Redux Toolkit slices
Use Zustand for lightweight global state in React apps
Review state patterns and choose the right tool per scenario
Configure Vite and understand module bundling
Deploy a React app to Vercel with CI/CD pipeline

challenge.title: ES6 Syntax Deep Dive
challenge.description: Explain destructuring, spread operator, and template literals with examples.

sessions.userResponse: Destructuring lets me unpack values from arrays and objects. Spread copies elements. Template literals use backticks for string interpolation. ES6 makes code much cleaner.
weaknesses: could go deeper on edge cases
sessions.feedback: Good foundation. Expand on rest parameters and nested destructuring.

challenge.title: Async/Await Mastery
challenge.description: Explain the event loop and demonstrate how async/await simplifies Promise chains.

sessions.userResponse: The event loop processes the call stack and task queue. Async functions return Promises automatically. Await pauses execution without blocking. I use try/catch for errors.
sessions.feedback: Strong grasp of async patterns. Study Promise.all for parallel operations.

challenge.title: Closures & Scope
challenge.description: Explain closures and demonstrate a practical use case in a React-like counter.

sessions.userResponse: A closure is a function that remembers its outer scope even after the outer function returns. This is how React hooks maintain state between renders — each call captures its own scope.
sessions.feedback: Excellent! The React hooks analogy shows deep understanding.

challenge.title: Event Loop Review
challenge.description: Walk through what happens when setTimeout(0) and a Promise.resolve() are both queued.

sessions.userResponse: Promise.resolve runs first because it's a microtask — microtasks drain completely before the macrotask queue. setTimeout goes to the macrotask queue so it runs after.
sessions.feedback: Perfect answer. You have mastered the JavaScript event loop.

challenge.title: JSX & Component Architecture
challenge.description: Explain JSX transformation and design a reusable Button component.

sessions.userResponse: JSX compiles to React.createElement calls. My Button takes variant, size, onClick props and spreads extras. It's reusable across the app via composition.
weaknesses: didn't mention key prop for lists
sessions.feedback: Strong component thinking. Remember key props when rendering lists.

challenge.title: Hooks: useState & useEffect
challenge.description: Build a data-fetching component using useState and useEffect with cleanup.

sessions.userResponse: I initialize useState with {data: null, loading: true}. useEffect fetches on mount with the URL in deps array. The cleanup returns a function to cancel the fetch with AbortController.
sessions.feedback: Excellent hooks usage. The AbortController pattern is production-ready.

challenge.title: Props & Component Composition
challenge.description: Design a Card component system using children prop and compound components.

sessions.userResponse: Card.Header, Card.Body, Card.Footer as compound components. The parent Card uses React.Children to compose. This avoids prop drilling and makes the API intuitive.
sessions.feedback: Outstanding. Compound components show senior-level React thinking.

challenge.title: React Router Navigation
challenge.description: Implement protected routes and nested routing for a dashboard app.

sessions.userResponse: I wrap the app in BrowserRouter. PrivateRoute checks auth state and redirects to /login if not authenticated using Navigate. Nested routes use Outlet for dashboard sections.
sessions.feedback: Perfect. This is exactly how production React apps handle routing.

challenge.title: Mini App: Task Manager
challenge.description: Architect a full task manager app with React — components, state, routing, and CRUD.

sessions.userResponse: TaskApp → TaskList → TaskItem + TaskForm. State lives in App with useState. Add/edit/delete handlers passed as props. React Router for /tasks and /tasks/:id routes.
weaknesses: could use useReducer for complex state
sessions.feedback: Excellent architecture. useReducer would scale better for this complexity.

challenge.title: Flexbox Layouts
challenge.description: Build a responsive navbar and card grid using only Flexbox.

sessions.userResponse: Navbar uses flex with justify-between for logo and links. Card grid uses flex-wrap with gap. Media queries swap flex-direction to column on mobile.
sessions.feedback: Solid Flexbox foundation. Study flex-grow and align-self for advanced layouts.

challenge.title: Tailwind CSS Mastery
challenge.description: Rebuild the card component from Day 5 using Tailwind utility classes only.

sessions.userResponse: Used bg-white dark:bg-slate-800 for dark mode, rounded-xl shadow-md p-4 for card. Hover states with hover:shadow-lg. md:grid-cols-3 for responsive grid.
sessions.feedback: Good Tailwind usage. Extract repeated patterns with @apply for maintainability.

challenge.title: Animations & Transitions
challenge.description: Add page transition and micro-interaction animations to the React app.

sessions.userResponse: CSS transition for button hover scale. Framer Motion AnimatePresence for route transitions. transform: translateY uses GPU acceleration so no layout thrash.
sessions.feedback: Strong animation understanding. will-change: transform hints the browser for better perf.

challenge.title: Context API Deep Dive
challenge.description: Build a theme and auth context system for a React app, avoiding unnecessary re-renders.

sessions.userResponse: Split ThemeContext and AuthContext into separate providers. Use useMemo for context values to prevent re-renders. Consumers only re-render when their specific context changes.
sessions.feedback: Good instinct on splitting contexts. Add useCallback for stable function references.

challenge.title: Redux Toolkit Slices
challenge.description: Build a shopping cart with Redux Toolkit — createSlice, async thunks, and selectors.

sessions.userResponse: cartSlice with addItem, removeItem, updateQuantity reducers. Immer lets me write mutating code that produces immutable state. createAsyncThunk for fetching products. createSelector for derived totals.
weaknesses: RTK Query would be better for data fetching
sessions.feedback: Solid Redux Toolkit grasp. Explore RTK Query to replace async thunks for API calls.

challenge.title: Zustand for Lightweight State
challenge.description: Compare Zustand vs Redux for a medium-complexity React app. Build a Zustand store.

sessions.userResponse: Zustand store with create() and slices for auth, cart, ui. Devtools middleware for debugging. Choose Zustand when Redux boilerplate outweighs benefit — solo or small team projects.
sessions.feedback: Good Zustand grasp. subscribeWithSelector allows fine-grained subscriptions.

challenge.title: State Patterns Review
challenge.description: For each scenario — form state, server cache, global auth, UI toggles — choose the best state tool.

sessions.userResponse: Forms → useState or React Hook Form. Server cache → React Query (TanStack Query). Global auth/cart → Zustand. UI toggles → useState locally. No single tool fits all.
sessions.feedback: Excellent mental model. You understand the right tool for the right job.

challenge.title: Vite Configuration
challenge.description: Configure Vite for a React app with path aliases, environment variables, and code splitting.

sessions.userResponse: vite.config with resolve.alias for @components, @utils. VITE_ prefix for env vars exposed to client. Dynamic import() for route-based code splitting. rollupOptions for chunk naming.
sessions.feedback: Good Vite basics. Study manualChunks to keep vendor bundles separate from app code.

challenge.title: Deploy to Vercel with CI/CD
challenge.description: Describe the full pipeline to deploy a React app with environment-specific configs and automatic preview deploys.

sessions.userResponse: Push to GitHub triggers Vercel CI. Preview deploys on every PR branch. Production deploys on main merge. Env vars set in Vercel dashboard per environment. vercel.json for rewrites to handle SPA routing.
sessions.feedback: Solid deployment workflow. Explore Vercel Edge Functions for API routes.

adaptations: Agent detected strong performance in React Fundamentals (avg 89%). Compressed review sessions to free 1 day.
State Management scored below threshold after Day 13. Added 1 extra practice session for Context API reinforcement.
Final sprint: All skills above 65%. Learner is on track to complete the full Frontend Developer roadmap.

agentDecisions.title: Goal Analyzed — Domain Detected
agentDecisions.detail: Identified "Frontend Development" as the learning domain. Learner level: intermediate, intensity: balanced.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Scanned goal text for domain-specific keywords. Found high-confidence matches: 'React' (score: 12), 'modern web apps' (score: 8), 'build' (score: 5). Matched frontend_development domain with 85% confidence. Detected tools: react, javascript, css.

agentDecisions.title: Skill Tree Constructed
agentDecisions.detail: Decomposed goal into 5 core skills spanning 18 learning days. Top priority: JavaScript Core, React Fundamentals, CSS & Styling.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Skills ranked by relevance to goal keywords and detected tools. JavaScript Core placed first — it is the prerequisite for all React work. React Fundamentals given most days (5) as it is the primary goal. Days allocated proportionally: foundational skills get more time, tooling gets less.

agentDecisions.title: Diagnostic Quiz Generated
agentDecisions.detail: Created 10 targeted questions across 5 skill areas to assess current proficiency before planning.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Questions selected by relevance score to the user's goal keywords. 2 questions per skill ensures fair coverage. Open-ended questions preferred over MCQ to capture reasoning depth, not just recall.

agentDecisions.title: Diagnostic Scored — Gaps Identified
agentDecisions.detail: 2 skill(s) need focused practice, 1 already proficient. Weakest: State Management (30%), React Fundamentals (45%).
agentDecisions.reasoning: Scored each answer via keyword matching and response depth. State Management at 30% — learner has no Redux experience. React Fundamentals at 45% — knows JSX but not hooks. CSS at 70% — already solid. Skills below 60% assigned extra learning days.

agentDecisions.title: Personalized Learning Plan Built
agentDecisions.detail: Generated 18-day plan. Weakest skills scheduled first to build strong foundations early.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Skills sorted by diagnostic score ascending: State Mgmt (30%) → React (45%) → Tooling (40%) → JS Core (62%) → CSS (70%). Wait — reordered by prerequisite chain instead: JS Core first (blocker), then React, then CSS (parallel), then State, then Tooling. Prerequisite ordering overrides score ordering.

agentDecisions.title: Day 4 Session Scored
agentDecisions.detail: Score: 90% (A). Strengths: microtask vs macrotask precise, correct ordering. Gaps: none.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Evaluated against 3 criteria. Full marks for microtask/macrotask distinction — a difficult concept most learners confuse. Response demonstrated conceptual understanding, not just memorization.

agentDecisions.title: Plan Adapted — React Fundamentals Accelerated
agentDecisions.detail: Agent detected strong performance in React Fundamentals (avg 89%). Compressed review sessions to free 1 day.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Agent monitors rolling average score every 3 sessions. React Fundamentals avg after 3 sessions: 89% — well above the 88% acceleration threshold. Removed 1 low-value review day. Learner is ready to progress faster.

agentDecisions.title: Day 8 Session Scored
agentDecisions.detail: Score: 95% (A+). Strengths: protected route pattern, Outlet usage, Navigate redirect. Gaps: none.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Evaluated against 4 criteria. Perfect execution of React Router v6 patterns. Learner demonstrated production-ready knowledge — this is above the expected level for an intermediate learner.

agentDecisions.title: Plan Adapted — State Management Reinforced
agentDecisions.detail: State Management scored below threshold after Day 13. Added 1 extra practice session for Context API reinforcement.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Context API session scored 68% — below the 70% continuation threshold. Agent inserted an additional practice session before moving to Redux. Learner needs more reps on useMemo and useCallback for context optimization before advanced state patterns.

agentDecisions.title: Day 16 Session Scored
agentDecisions.detail: Score: 80% (B+). Strengths: React Query mention, correct per-scenario choices. Gaps: none.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Evaluated against 4 criteria. The React Query recommendation shows awareness of the modern state management ecosystem beyond basic Redux. Learner has developed a nuanced mental model.

agentDecisions.title: Day 18 Session Complete — Journey Finished
agentDecisions.detail: Score: 70% (B-). Full 18-day plan completed. All 5 skills assessed and graded.
agentDecisions.reasoning: Final session completes the learning journey. Overall trajectory: started at 30-62% diagnostic → finished at 67-91% mastery. Consistent upward trend across all skills confirms the adaptive plan worked. Learner is ready to build production React apps.

report.goalText: I want to learn React and build modern web apps
report.narrative: SkillForge tracked 18 practice sessions over 18 days and found consistent upward progression across all 5 Frontend Development skills. The learner started with significant gaps in State Management (30%) and React Fundamentals (45%), but through the adaptive plan, finished at 74% and 91% respectively — a dramatic improvement. React Router was a particular strength, scoring 95% (A+). The agent made 2 plan adaptations: accelerating React Fundamentals after exceptional early performance, and adding a reinforcement session when State Management dipped below threshold. The overall average of 80% across 18 sessions reflects a learner who engages deeply and improves consistently.
report.coachSummary: Exceptional progress across the board. React Fundamentals and JavaScript Core are production-ready. Focus next on deepening State Management — specifically RTK Query and context optimization — and Frontend Tooling with advanced Vite configuration. You are ready to build and ship real-world React applications.
adaptations: Agent detected strong performance in React Fundamentals (avg 89%). Compressed review sessions to free 1 day.
State Management scored below threshold after Day 13. Added 1 extra practice session for Context API reinforcement.
Final sprint: All skills above 65%. Learner is on track to complete the full Frontend Developer roadmap.
